title: Hypoglycemia review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.
red_flags.rationale: Reviewed for profound hypoglycaemia context; no emergency conclusion or automatic referral item is mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

investigations.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped adult type 2 diabetes home-glucose, HbA1c, and hypoglycaemia-awareness review fields with scope qualifiers retained.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

assessment.text: Defines clinically relevant glucose and severe-event context, including events requiring assistance and possible loss of consciousness or seizure, without diagnosing an individual event.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped event identity, glucose-value, severe-assistance, loss-of-consciousness, and seizure documentation.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines clinically relevant glucose and severe-event context, including events requiring assistance and possible loss of consciousness or seizure, without diagnosing an individual event.

source.title: 6. Glycemic Goals, Hypoglycemia, and Hyperglycemic Crises: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

exact_section.heading: Hypoglycemia definitions and event rates; Table 6.4
exact_section.locator: Hypoglycemia Definitions and Event Rates; Table 6.4

medication.text: Supports reviewing hypoglycaemia history at encounters, awareness, high-risk or severe events, clinician-led treatment-plan reevaluation, structured education, and glucagon discussion without inserting treatment instructions.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-led treatment-plan review, follow-up, education, and glucagon-discussion documentation without treatment instructions.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports reviewing hypoglycaemia history at encounters, awareness, high-risk or severe events, clinician-led treatment-plan reevaluation, structured education and glucagon discussion without inserting treatment instructions.

source.title: 6. Glycemic Goals, Hypoglycemia, and Hyperglycemic Crises: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

medication.text: Supports documenting event frequency, severity, precipitants, symptoms, treatment approach, glucose readings, medication context, awareness, and risk factors.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped episode frequency, timing, symptoms, medication, meal or activity, awareness, glucose, and history-draft documentation.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting event frequency, severity, precipitants, symptoms, treatment approach, glucose readings, medication context, awareness and risk factors.

source.title: 6. Glycemic Goals, Hypoglycemia, and Hyperglycemic Crises: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

medication.text: Supports meal, activity, illness, monitoring, driving-safety, education, and glucagon context as clinician-reviewed documentation only.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped driving-safety and monitoring context without generating advice.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports meal, activity, illness, monitoring, driving-safety, education and glucagon context as clinician-reviewed documentation only.

source.title: 6. Glycemic Goals, Hypoglycemia, and Hyperglycemic Crises: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

source_limitations: The legacy workflow does not encode diabetes type, age, pregnancy, medicine class, or all high-risk qualifiers.
Alcohol context, broad vitals, general appearance, weight, blood pressure, generic clinician plan, safety-net wording, fixed follow-up intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, emergency response, glucose correction, treatment, medicine selection or dose, driving restriction, and referral remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
